RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LYNN KLOYTHANOMSUP, ANDREW LEONIDAS, SAMUEL GONZALEZ-RAMIREZ's Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendants.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Denied without prejudice as the motion is untimely due to the closing of discovery for the July 26, 2021, trial date. Written discovery closes 30 days before trial which is June 26, 2021. Written discovery must be served by hand no later than 60 days before trial which is May 27, 2021-15 days prior to the hearing date. Plaintiff's moving papers also fail to provide the actual discovery sought (i.e., document requests, subpoenas etc.) which makes it difficult for the court to anticipate the scope of the discovery plaintiff seeks. The court also finds that plaintiffs have not met their burden under Civil Code section 3295(c) that there is a "substantial probability that the plaintiff[s] will prevail" on their claims of punitive damages as the court finds that there are contrary stories in the declarations. Plaintiffs may seek post trial discovery on defendants if they prevail on their punitive damages claims.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 to the Court at klgallo@discoveryreferee.com.
